title: Artemis II Crew Holds Postflight News Conference at Johnson Space Center
detail: The four Artemis II astronauts — Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, Mission Specialist Christina Koch, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen — held their official postflight news conference at NASA's Johnson Space Center in Houston at 2:30 p.m. EDT. The crew discussed their historic 10-day lunar flyby mission, including the lunar far side photography, manual spacecraft piloting in deep space, and the emotional experience of becoming the farthest-traveling humans in history at 252,706 miles from Earth. The astronauts reflected on the psychological impact of viewing Earth from lunar distance and spoke to what the mission's success means for the Artemis IV crewed lunar landing, targeted for 2028.

sources.name: NASA News Release: Artemis II Crew Postflight News Conference
WKNO FM: NASA's Artemis II News Conference with Moon Astronauts

media.caption: Artemis II crew at postflight news conference at Johnson Space Center, April 16, 2026

title: Mobile Launcher 1 Rolls from LC-39B to VAB — Artemis III Stacking Preparations Begin
detail: NASA's Mobile Launcher 1 began rolling from Launch Complex 39B back to the Vehicle Assembly Building at 8:11 a.m. EDT on Crawler-Transporter 2, completing approximately a 4-mile trek. The relocation follows the successful conclusion of the Artemis II mission and clears the launch pad for Artemis III preparations. Teams will perform essential checkouts, inspections, and repairs on ML-1 before stacking SLS and Orion for the Artemis III mission, which is redesignated as a low-Earth orbit rendezvous and docking test and targeted for launch next year. The rollout marks the formal transition from Artemis II post-flight operations to Artemis III launch campaign activities.

sources.name: NASA Artemis Blog: Mobile Launcher Rolls Ahead of Artemis III Preparation

media.caption: NASA's Mobile Launcher 1 rolls from LC-39B to VAB for Artemis III preparation, April 16, 2026
